threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused at the bus stops, waiting for a 38 instead of the 149 or 277—classic rookie move. They don’t run in sync, you’ll learn.
Caught a late 159 last week. Some bloke serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B, but the driver was unamused, gave him the silent treatment.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy in flip-flops arguing with a pigeon over a kebab at Hermannplatz. That’s when it hit me, I’m home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you sip a beer while watching a spontaneous techno party take over an abandoned building. It's like urban art meets nightlife.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the BART delays—grabbing a burrito from La Taqueria while checking their watch, no panic in sight.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy trying to sell a parrot on the 38 Geary. Parrot was expertly imitating the bus announcements. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume that all Gràcia bars are tapas spots, but some of the best are actually dedicated to craft cocktails or beer. They'll never find the hidden gems.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered expression at the metro ticket machines—still trying to figure out how to buy a T-10 without a guide.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still take the bus when the Tube's down. Locals just walk or cycle, strike chaos is a way of life here.
Heard a guy on the 196 last night talking about his cats like they were his kids. Serious debate over who gets the best spot on the sofa.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a couple arguing passionately in Turkish over the price of fresh produce at the market, I realized how alive this place really is.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you find a cozy café amidst an old bingo hall, where locals sip coffee while chatting about last night's match. Total vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to dodge the BART delays and still be ten minutes early to a taco spot. Tourists wear watches; we check train apps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 Geary crammed with tourists, but the driver preaches about local gems like it's a guided tour. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume every bar serves tapas at any hour, but many local spots only do it before 9 PM. After that, it’s drinks only.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the tram system; they always seem surprised when it comes to a sudden halt, like it’s a game.
